lede: A look at October 15 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Virgil and Temür Khan, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Kidnapping of Jayme Closs
events.desc: 13-year-old American girl, Jayme Closs, is kidnapped from her Barron, Wisconsin home after her parents were both murdered.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: A draft card is burned during an anti-war rally by the Catholic Worker Movement, resulting in the first arrest under a new law.

events.title: Hurricane Hazel
events.desc: Hurricane Hazel devastates the eastern seaboard of North America, killing 95 and causing massive floods as far north as Toronto.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Germany replaces the Hungarian government after Hungary announces an armistice with the Soviet Union.

events.title: Lluís Companys
events.desc: President Lluís Companys of Catalonia is executed by the Francoist government.

events.title: Air India
events.desc: Tata Airlines (later to become Air India) makes its first flight.

events.title: From Hell letter
events.desc: The "From Hell" letter allegedly sent by Jack the Ripper is received by investigators.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The Union garrison of Glasgow, Missouri surrenders to Confederate forces.

events.title: Napoleon
events.desc: Napoleon begins his exile on Saint Helena in the South Atlantic Ocean.

events.title: Qing dynasty
events.desc: Qing forces capture the island of Zhoushan. Zhu Yihai, Prince of Lu, resident of the island and regent of the Southern Ming, flees to Kinmen.

births.name: A. P. J. Abdul Kalam
births.line: Indian engineer, academic, and politician, 11th President of India

deaths.text: Konrad Emil Bloch, Polish-American biochemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Hermann Göring, German general and Nazi politician, convicted Nuremburg war criminal
Lluís Companys, Catalan lawyer and politician, President of Catalonia
